title: A general existence proof for non-linear elliptic equations in   semi-Riemannian spaces
comment:
18 pages, v3: minor corrections, final version, to appear in Am. J.
  Math
summary:
We present a general existence proof for a wide class of non-linear elliptic
equations which can be applied to problems with barrier conditions without
specifying any assumptions guaranteeing the uniqueness or local uniqueness of
particular solutions.
  As an application we prove the existence of closed hypersurfaces with
curvature prescribed in the tangent bundle of an ambient Riemannian manifold
$N$ without supposing any sign condition on the sectional curvatures $K_N$. A
curvature flow wouldn't work in this situation, neither the method of
successive approximation.
